creator: sroberts@internet-mail.org
text:
FreeBAS machine does not stay up more than a week without rebooting with a kernel panic.

The day after each reboot, I see the following message:

panic: kmem_malloc(11186176): kmem_map too small: 315637760 total allocated

Obviously the numbers vary for each case.

Here is the /var/run/dmesg.boot output:

TCP: [127.0.0.1]:58511 to [127.0.0.1]:113 tcpflags 0x2<SYN>; tcp_input: Connection attempt to closed port
Connection attempt to UDP 127.0.0.1:512 from 127.0.0.1:50106
Connection attempt to UDP 127.0.0.1:512 from 127.0.0.1:62833
TCP: [127.0.0.1]:60351 to [127.0.0.1]:113 tcpflags 0x2<SYN>; tcp_input: Connection attempt to closed port

Connection attempt to UDP 127.0.0.1:512 from 127.0.0.1:50493
Connection attempt to UDP 127.0.0.1:512 from 127.0.0.1:55798
panic: kmem_malloc(11186176): kmem_map too small: 315637760 total allocated
cpuid = 0

How-To-Repeat: Leave FreeBSD machine running
